About IRO: International Relations Office (IRO) at The North Cap University, Gurugram, heralds a transformative chapter as it transcends geographical confines to forge robust ties with global academia. Serving as a nexus between NCU and esteemed international institutions, the IRO propels the university onto the global stage, enriching the educational experience for students, scholars, and faculty alike. Embracing a philosophy of global interconnectedness, the IRO cultivates a vibrant multicultural community, preparing individuals to navigate the complexities of the global landscape while fostering transcontinental collaborations. Our commitment to erasing boundaries and broadening horizons underscores our mission to provide unparalleled educational opportunities, positioning NCU amongst the global elite. E-Mail: deaninternational@ncuindia.edu

FAQs: What is the difference between Study in India (SII) Scholarship and Tuition Fee Waivers? | Study in India (SII) Scholarship covers tuition fee, accommodation and canteen charges upto USD 3500. Tuition fee waivers are concessions given by institutes on the tuition fees in the ranges of 100%, 50% & 25%. The rest of the charges have to be paid by the student. For more details, visit https://www.studyinindia.gov.in/

B.Tech CSE, Batch of 2020 My Trip to UEU Jakarta was filled with adventures ,learnings and memories.I would recommend everyone to join such immersion programs to broaden their horizons where learning meets fun and equals cherishing memories B.Tech EECE Diving into Japan with NCU was an adrenaline-packed adventure that blended cultural depth with cutting-edge innovation. It kicked off in Shibuya, buzzing with arcade fervor, before shifting to Akihabara, a haven for tech and anime. At Shibaura University of Technology, collaboration was key. I teamed up with Japanese peers on Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), turning a presentation into a cross-cultural dialogue. Donning a yukata added a traditional twist, linking past traditions with modern vibrancy. The trip included a visit to the historical Edo Museum and the futuristic Kawasaki Robotic Museum, each stop revealing a facet of Japan's dynamic ethos. A serene day at Omiya campus offered a peaceful contrast to Tokyo's hustle. Evenings were spent exploring Ginza’s anime scenes, like "Spy x Family" and "Kaguya-sama: Love Is War". On foot, we conquered Tokyo, racking up 30,000 steps daily, fully immersed in the city's unstoppable rhythm. This journey was a relentless dive into the vibrant heart of Japan, leaving me exhilarated and eager for more. Student Testimonials: Isheeta Sehgal | B.Tech CSE, Batch of 2024 Went for a summer immersion at SIT Japan. Visiting Japan has been a long dream of mine, I’m grateful to The Northcap University to make it come true. It was a truely an unforgettable experience. From exploring Tokyo as a student to meeting students at Toyosu and Omiya campus. This program provided an immersive experience of not only the life in Tokyo but also how advance research labs and students are at Shibaura Institute of Technology. This experience helped me gain a deeper understanding of Japanese traditions, food and way of life. Meeting people from different countries who were part of SIT exchange program pursing their studies in Tokyo, discussing with them about social issues present in the world and coming up with solutions, going on industrial visits with them enriched my perspective. I will always cherish the memories made on this immersion program with the students of NCU and SIT.

B.Tech CSE, Batch of 2020 My Trip to UEU Jakarta was filled with adventures ,learnings and memories.I would recommend everyone to join such immersion programs to broaden their horizons where learning meets fun and equals cherishing memories B.Tech EECE Diving into Japan with NCU was an adrenaline-packed adventure that blended cultural depth with cutting-edge innovation. It kicked off in Shibuya, buzzing with arcade fervor, before shifting to Akihabara, a haven for tech and anime. At Shibaura University of Technology, collaboration was key. I teamed up with Japanese peers on Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), turning a presentation into a cross-cultural dialogue. Donning a yukata added a traditional twist, linking past traditions with modern vibrancy. The trip included a visit to the historical Edo Museum and the futuristic Kawasaki Robotic Museum, each stop revealing a facet of Japan's dynamic ethos. A serene day at Omiya campus offered a peaceful contrast to Tokyo's hustle. Evenings were spent exploring Ginza’s anime scenes, like "Spy x Family" and "Kaguya-sama: Love Is War". On foot, we conquered Tokyo, racking up 30,000 steps daily, fully immersed in the city's unstoppable rhythm. This journey was a relentless dive into the vibrant heart of Japan, leaving me exhilarated and eager for more. Student Testimonials: Yajin Vadehra | B.Tech EECE Went for a summer immersion at SIT Japan. 2023 Diving into Japan with NCU was an adrenaline-packed adventure that blended cultural depth with cutting-edge innovation. It kicked off in Shibuya, buzzing with arcade fervor, before shifting to Akihabara, a haven for tech and anime. At Shibaura University of Technology, collaboration was key. I teamed up with Japanese peers on Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), turning a presentation into a cross-cultural dialogue. Donning a yukata added a traditional twist, linking past traditions with modern vibrancy. The trip included a visit to the historical Edo Museum and the futuristic Kawasaki Robotic Museum, each stop revealing a facet of Japan's dynamic ethos. A serene day at Omiya campus offered a peaceful contrast to Tokyo's hustle. Evenings were spent exploring Ginza’s anime scenes, like "Spy x Family" and "Kaguya-sama: Love Is War". On foot, we conquered Tokyo, racking up 30,000 steps daily, fully immersed in the city's unstoppable rhythm. This journey was a relentless dive into the vibrant heart of Japan, leaving me exhilarated and eager for more.
